quick_skim.title: Multimodal Concept Bottleneck Models
quick_skim.abstract: Concept Bottleneck Models (CBMs) enhance the interpretability of deep learning networks by aligning the features extracted from images with natural concepts. However, existing CBMs are constrained in their ability to generalize beyond a fixed set of predefined classes and the risk of non-concept information leakage, where predictive signals outside the intended concepts are inadvertently exploited. In this paper, we propose Multimodal Concept Bottleneck Model (MM-CBM) to address these issues and extend CBMs into CLIP. MM-CBM utilizes dual Concept Bottleneck Layers (CBLs) to align both the image and text embeddings into interpretable features. This allows us to perform new vision tasks like zero-shot classification or image retrieval in an interpretable way. Compared to existing methods, MM-CBM achieves up to 51.26% accuracy improvement on average across four standard benchmarks. Our method maintains high accuracy, staying within ~5% of black-box performance while offering greater interpretability.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multimodal vision-language model for interpretable classification transferable to food nutrition prediction
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多模态视觉语言模型方法可迁移至食品营养预测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多模态视觉语言模型方法可迁移至食品营养预测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多模态视觉语言模型方法可迁移至食品营养预测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Extends concept bottleneck models to multimodal CLIP framework using dual bottleneck layers for interpretable image-text alignment, enabling zero-shot classification and retrieval.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有概念瓶颈模型泛化能力有限且存在非概念信息泄漏的问题，本文提出了多模态概念瓶颈模型（MM-CBM），将概念瓶颈模型扩展到CLIP框架中。MM-CBM通过双重概念瓶颈层同时对图像和文本嵌入进行可解释对齐，实现了零样本分类和图像检索等新视觉任务。实验结果表明，该方法在保持可解释性的同时，有效提升了模型的泛化能力。该工作为多模态可解释人工智能提供了新思路，可迁移至食品营养预测等需要跨模态对齐的应用场景。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对现有概念瓶颈模型泛化能力有限且存在非概念信息泄漏的问题，本文提出了多模态概念瓶颈模型（MM-CBM），将概念瓶颈模型扩展到CLIP框架中。MM-CBM通过双重概念瓶颈层同时对图像和文本嵌入进行可解释对齐，实现了零样本分类和图像检索等新视觉任务。实验结果表明，该方法在保持可解释性的同时，有效提升了模型的泛化能力。该工作为多模态可解释人工智能提供了新思路，可迁移至食品营养预测等需要跨模态对齐的应用场景。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: multimodal vision language model food recognition nutrition
